summary: Every relationship can change one user's follower count, so exact output has a linear row-inspection lower bound; one grouped scan matches it.

optimality.argument: Changing any uninspected relationship changes the count for its user or introduces another represented user, so all R rows must be considered. Grouping by user_id updates one counter per row in expected constant time, and the composite primary key begins with user_id so an ordered index scan can aggregate groups while preserving the required ascending output.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary may place the only relationship for a user or one additional follower for an existing user in any uninspected row.
One GROUP BY scan counts each primary-key relationship once and emits groups in user_id order through the declared composite-key ordering.
Authored fixtures cover the public sample, one row, one large group, unsorted input, nonconsecutive users, a follower shared across users, and zero and large identifiers.
